title: Bridge Essay: Visual imagery during real-time fMRI neurofeedback from occipital and superior parietal cortex.
concept: Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.
**Phase context:** Mental imagery as real perception — imagination, pareidolia, aphantasia.

**Visual imagery during real-time fMRI neurofeedback from occipital and superior parietal cortex.** — Neuroimage (2019). DOI: 10.1016/j.neuroimage.2019.06.057. PMID: 31247298. Visual imagery has been suggested to recruit occipital cortex via feedback projections from fronto-parietal regions, suggesting that these feedback projections might be exploited to boost recruitment of occipital cortex by means of real-time neurofeedback. To test this prediction, we instructed a group of healthy participants to perform peripheral visual imagery while they received real-time auditory feedback based on the BOLD signal from either early visual cortex or the medial superior parieta
